pi_items.source: setup_task.sh defines PROJECT_DIR as this path, establishing it as the target directory for the OpenToonz project.
task.json description states to save the scene as this path, and export_result.sh checks for this specific PROJECT_FILE.
setup_task.sh and export_result.sh define and check OUTPUT_DIR using this path for rendered frames.
verifier.py uses this value as the minimal z-depth difference threshold, although task.json description asks the user to position a level at Z > 300.
task.json description specifies rendering a 20-frame sequence, and verifier.py checks if the number of rendered frames is >= 20.

privileged_info_summary: The task requires creating an OpenToonz multiplane camera project in the directory '/home/ga/OpenToonz/projects/multiplane', saving the scene file precisely at '/home/ga/OpenToonz/projects/multiplane/multiplane.tnz', and exporting rendered frames to '/home/ga/OpenToonz/output/multiplane'. The task mandates creating an animation with a minimum of 20 rendered frames, and while the description advises a Z-depth separation of > 300, the programmatic minimum required by the verifier is a Z-depth difference of 200.
